hyperedges.label: Le contrat d'extraction — ce que extractCards et build.js scrutent ensemble
Les trois blocs :root du carnet — charte partagée / rampe locale / colonne, jamais fusionnés
Extraction positionnelle des trois tables (Verbes ≥5, Adjectifs ≥4, Noms ≥3 colonnes)
Provenance du build standalone (app.html + carnet → build.js → fichier généré)
Flux de révision espacée (Leitner, srs_v1)
Flux de réponse du mode Saisie (vérification tolérante + verdict corrigeable)
Flux de démarrage (fetch du carnet → extraction → construction de l'accueil)
Les règles nommées de la charte visuelle
Le couplage critique des deux extracteurs et ses gardes
Le workflow d'économie de contexte (graphe + sous-agents + recalage conditionnel)

nodes.norm_label: design.md — nuit d'encre / or ancien token system

nodes.norm_label: nuit d'encre background + or ancien glyph

nodes.label: Same golden aleph used as vector glyph on the portal welcome screen
nodes.norm_label: same golden aleph used as vector glyph on the portal welcome screen

nodes.label: Les trois modes : Cartes, Saisie, QCM
nodes.rationale: Cartes recto-verso avec auto-évaluation et annulation ; Saisie tolérante (translittération à la française ou vrai hébreu, clavier virtuel réservé au bureau, « Presque ! » à une faute près) ; QCM à 4 choix sans quasi-synonymes, jouable au clavier 1-4.
nodes.norm_label: les trois modes : cartes, saisie, qcm

nodes.label: Révision du jour (répétition espacée, vue utilisateur)
nodes.rationale: Chaque réponse dans n'importe quel mode fait monter ou descendre la carte ; les échues se regroupent en session tous thèmes ; la progression survit entre les sessions ; après une révision, les ratées redeviennent dues aussitôt — c'est la méthode, pas un bug.
nodes.norm_label: revision du jour (repetition espacee, vue utilisateur)

nodes.label: PWA installable et hors ligne (vue utilisateur)
nodes.rationale: iPhone : Safari → Sur l'écran d'accueil ; icône א dorée, plein écran, fonctionne hors ligne ; les mises à jour poussées sur main sont visibles au lancement suivant ; une icône déjà installée garde le comportement de son installation.
nodes.norm_label: pwa installable et hors ligne (vue utilisateur)

nodes.label: Mise à jour automatique depuis le carnet
nodes.rationale: Les cartes ne sont pas figées : app.html lit le carnet au chargement, donc modifier le carnet suffit pour que les flashcards se mettent à jour ; seule la version autonome exige node build.js.
nodes.norm_label: mise a jour automatique depuis le carnet

nodes.label: vocabulaire_hebreu.html — Le carnet de vocabulaire (source de vérité)
nodes.rationale: Single source of truth for all content (757 cards). Static self-contained HTML: inline CSS, three small inline scripts, no dependencies. app.html fetches it at runtime; build.js re-parses it with regexes.
nodes.norm_label: vocabulaire_hebreu.html — le carnet de vocabulaire (source de verite)

nodes.label: :root #1 — Jetons de charte partagés (Nuit d'encre / Or ancien)
nodes.rationale: Must stay byte-identical across vocabulaire_hebreu.html, app.html and index.html (the notebook is the reference, DESIGN.md §6). Defines --bg #12181f, --gold #d4a24c, --ink, --green, --red, --line. If --bg/--gold change, icons and manifest theme-color must be regenerated.
nodes.norm_label: :root #1 — jetons de charte partages (nuit d'encre / or ancien)

nodes.label: :root #2 — Rampe typographique à 8 pas (locale au carnet)
nodes.rationale: Eight steps from --pas-titre 2.3rem to --pas-micro 0.7rem; no literal font-size may remain outside it (sole exception: relative em on Hebrew in French prose). CRITICAL TRAP documented inline: 1rem = 16px, NOT 22px — font-size:22px sits on body and does not move the rem root; the old comment claiming otherwise was false and caused a 24-value type drift. Do not move the 22px to html: it would scale every rem by 1.375 here and in app.html.
nodes.norm_label: :root #2 — rampe typographique a 8 pas (locale au carnet)

nodes.label: :root #3 — Colonne de lecture (--colonne 28rem / --colonne-large 56rem)
nodes.rationale: Both values are MEASURED, not deduced: --colonne-large 56rem is a floor (widest table sits at 890px; below 55.6rem, 7 of 29 tables gain a horizontal scrollbar — tried, measured, reverted 2026-07-20); --colonne 28rem is calibrated on real prose advance (6.63px/char in Assistant 16px), never on a digit's width (the '0' is 7.87px, 19% too wide). Implemented via padding-inline on main, not max-width+auto margins, to preserve child margin collapsing. The unbounded width defect (158 chars/line at 1280) survived every critique because visual checks were iPhone-emulated.

nodes.label: Contrat de balisage du carnet (ce que les deux extracteurs scrutent)
nodes.rationale: The DOM/text shape that app.html extractCards() (DOM scraping) and build.js (regex re-implementation) both depend on. Any edit to the notebook that breaks a component of this contract silently drops cards; build.js --check byte-compares the two extractors to catch drift.
nodes.norm_label: contrat de balisage du carnet (ce que les deux extracteurs scrutent)

nodes.label: Convention h2 > span.count — le libellé exact est la clé de section
nodes.rationale:
28 sections are identified by

containing LABEL. The exact LABEL text ('Verbes', 'Adjectifs', 'Noms', 'Pronoms personnels', 'Nombres (0–10)'…) is the extraction key: renaming a count label detaches its section from card extraction.
nodes.norm_label: convention h2 > span.count — le libelle exact est la cle de section

nodes.label: Balisage ul.word-list > li (sections en liste)
nodes.rationale: Each <li> carries span.word-main > span.he[lang=he] plus span.meta > span.tr + span.fr. Nested <li> (examples inside an entry) break naive regexes: build.js lisOf does depth-aware top-level scanning, app.html uses the ul.word-list > li direct-child selector — both properties must be preserved.

nodes.label: Balisage des tables Verbes/Adjectifs/Noms (extraction positionnelle)
nodes.rationale: Entries live in <div class="table-wrap"><table><tbody><tr>. Extraction is positional: Verbes needs ≥5 columns (Infinitif/MS/FS/MP/FP), Adjectifs ≥4, Noms ≥3 (col 2 = genre m/f as bare td text, col 3 = plural). Fields come from child spans .he/.tr/.fr; the word's own .he/.fr must come FIRST in the cell — extraction reads the first .he/.fr of the fragment, before ul.exemples.

nodes.label: Balisage ul.exemples (exemple obligatoire par entrée de table)
nodes.rationale: Every Noms/Adjectifs/Verbes entry must carry at least one <ul class="exemples"> (verbs: a present-tense sentence) nested inside the first td, after the word's own .he/.fr spans. verifie_exemples.js treats a missing ul.exemples as a blocking error.
nodes.norm_label: balisage ul.exemples (exemple obligatoire par entree de table)

nodes.label: Attribut data-niveau (niveau CECR obligatoire)
nodes.rationale: Mandatory on every <li> of ul.word-list and every <tr> of the three tables (A1/A2/B1…). build.js fails naming the offending word if any card comes out without one (coverage 757/757). The app's tolerance for unclassified cards is a safety net, not a licence.

nodes.label: Convention lang="he" sur tout nœud hébreu
nodes.rationale: 100% of ~5234 Hebrew nodes carry lang="he" (measured in-browser 2026-07-21). The count must be MEASURED in a browser, not computed from source: each entry's .he also spawns a generated span.cursive at runtime, so one word is worth more than one node.
nodes.norm_label: convention lang="he" sur tout nœud hebreu

nodes.label: Triplet de spans .he / .tr / .fr
nodes.rationale: The atomic data cell of the whole notebook: Hebrew with nikud (.he, lang=he), transliteration (.tr, follows the project standard: kh/ch/ts, apostrophe for ayin, ei for tsere…), French gloss (.fr). Used identically in word-list items, table cells and example sentences; both extractors and the in-page search read these class names.

nodes.label: Script de génération des lignes cursives (stripNikud)
nodes.rationale: On DOMContentLoaded, for each td .he and .word-main .he, inserts a span.cursive[lang=he] with nikud stripped (U+0591–U+05C7). Deliberately restricted to dictionary entries, never running prose, where a display:block line would fragment the sentence. This runtime generation is why the lang=he node count can only be measured in a browser.
nodes.norm_label: script de generation des lignes cursives (stripnikud)

nodes.label: Script de recherche / filtrage dynamique du carnet
nodes.rationale: Indexes ul.word-list > li, table tbody tr and .example blocks (normalizing accents + nikud), hides non-matching entries/sections/tables/parts, highlights matches in .fr/.tr/.ex-fr/.ex-tr, debounced 150ms. Deliberately excludes .cursive from the haystack. Uses :scope > li so hidden entries' nested example <li> don't keep sections visible (critique 21/07, P1). Hides the TOC and section prose while filtering — 'une recherche montre des correspondances, pas des leçons'.
nodes.norm_label: script de recherche / filtrage dynamique du carnet

nodes.label: Script de bascule du lien flashcards en protocole file:
nodes.rationale: app.html cannot fetch the notebook under file://; when location.protocol is file:, the header link #app-link is rewritten from ./app.html to ./flashcards_hebreu.html (the standalone, which works everywhere).
nodes.norm_label: script de bascule du lien flashcards en protocole file:

nodes.label: Sommaire (nav.toc) — 28 sections en 6 groupes
nodes.rationale: Complete (28/28 sections) and split into short groups after the 21/07 critique flagged 6 sections without a pill (including Phrases, the content PRODUCT.md designates as the goal) and one 12-pill run. Pill order STRICTLY follows document order. Anchors target the h2 ids (#sec-…).

nodes.label: Section Verbes (tables 5 colonnes, présent MS/FS/MP/FP)
nodes.rationale: Largest section, thematic subthemes (Mouvement & déplacement, Vie quotidienne…). Its widest table (Verbes · Vie quotidienne) sets the 890px floor behind --colonne-large. Card extraction requires ≥5 columns; the present-tense forms become the card's forms[] array.

nodes.label: Section Noms (tables 3 colonnes : singulier / genre / pluriel)
nodes.rationale: Column 2 is the genre as bare td text (m/f — the only cell without spans, indexed specially by the search script), column 3 the plural. Extraction needs ≥3 columns.
nodes.norm_label: section noms (tables 3 colonnes : singulier / genre / pluriel)

nodes.label: Section Adjectifs (tables 4 colonnes)
nodes.rationale: Four-form agreement tables (MS/FS/MP/FP without infinitive column); extraction needs ≥4 columns.

nodes.label: Sections en liste (listCats) — pronoms, mots-outils, nombres, phrases…
nodes.rationale: All non-table card sections: Pronoms personnels, Démonstratifs, Verbes modaux, Prépositions, Conjonctions, Mots interrogatifs, Nombres (0–10), Nombres (11 et plus), Nombres ordinaux, Jours de la semaine, Mots de quantité, Adverbes, Saisons & mois, Expressions / Divers, Phrases. Each uses ul.word-list; adding one requires mirroring in build.js (listCats AND EXPECTED_CATS).
nodes.norm_label: sections en liste (listcats) — pronoms, mots-outils, nombres, phrases...

nodes.label: Sections de grammaire pure (exclues des cartes)
nodes.rationale: La racine, Le passé, Le futur, Patrons de conjugaison (binyanim), L'article défini, État construit (smikhut), Prépositions fléchies, Le hé directionnel, Existence et possession, Le « que » de subordination. They carry h2 count labels but have no .count→category mapping in the extractors: intentionally excluded from cards, they are the 'cours' half of the notebook (Partie 1).
nodes.norm_label: sections de grammaire pure (exclues des cartes)

nodes.label: Anneau de focus doré (:focus-visible, idiome des trois fichiers)
nodes.rationale: outline:2px solid var(--gold); outline-offset:2px — common idiom across the three files (app.html is the reference). Never a border-radius here: it would not decorate the ring, it would redraw the focused element (DESIGN.md §5).
nodes.norm_label: anneau de focus dore (:focus-visible, idiome des trois fichiers)

nodes.label: Chargement non bloquant des Google Fonts (media=print → all)
nodes.rationale: The stylesheet loads with media="print" then flips to 'all' onload, with a <noscript> fallback: as a classic <link>, WebKit paints NOTHING — not even the background — until the sheet arrives, which was the whole 'slow first paint' of the very first load. Fonts: Frank Ruhl Libre (Hebrew serif), Assistant (body), JetBrains Mono, Playpen Sans Hebrew (cursive).
nodes.norm_label: chargement non bloquant des google fonts (media=print → all)

nodes.label: Règle :has(+ .table-wrap) — le filet suit le cadre qu'il annonce
nodes.rationale: A subtheme title takes the wide frame only if it carries a rule AND opens onto a framed object (both conditions): a 448px rule above an 896px table reads as misalignment (17 cases), and the same rule widened above a plain list is the same defect reversed (4 cases). main > *:not(.table-wrap) weighs 0,1,1 and acts as a specificity FLOOR: any bare-element selector contradicting it is silently ignored — a dead rule shipped with a comment claiming it worked on 2026-07-20. The :has() weighs 0,2,1 and wins on its own specificity. Without :has() support, subthemes stay on the column (pre-existing state, no breakage; WebKit has it since 15.4).
nodes.norm_label: regle :has(+ .table-wrap) — le filet suit le cadre qu'il annonce

nodes.label: Convention .empty — le pointillé ne dit que « rien ici »
nodes.rationale: border dashed means ONLY 'nothing here' (.empty) in this notebook; every other rule is solid (warnings use .attention). The empty state was dead CSS since its creation — the search counter's copy promised a void the screen never showed — until the 21/07 critique (P1) had the search script actually instantiate the .empty element.
nodes.norm_label: convention .empty — le pointille ne dit que « rien ici »

nodes.label: flashcards_hebreu.html — Standalone hors-ligne (fichier généré)
nodes.source_location: line 2 (banner): FICHIER GÉNÉRÉ par `node build.js` depuis app.html + vocabulaire_hebreu.html — ne pas éditer à la main
nodes.rationale: 100% generated artifact: hand edits are erased on the next `node build.js` run (trap #1 of CLAUDE.md). Exists so the flashcards work as a plain file:// with no network — the online app.html must fetch the notebook over HTTP, this build embeds everything.

nodes.label: Instantané de cartes intégré (const CARDS)
nodes.source_location: line 785: const CARDS = [{"cat":"Verbes",...}] — full card array inlined as JSON
nodes.rationale: Replaces app.html's runtime fetch of the notebook: build.js runs its regex extractor over vocabulaire_hebreu.html and serializes the resulting card objects here. Property insertion order must stay identical to app.html's extractCards — `node build.js --check` byte-compares this snapshot to catch extractor drift.

nodes.label: Retrait de la clôture BUILD:ONLINE-ONLY
nodes.source_location: whole file: 0 occurrences of BUILD:ONLINE-ONLY, 0 occurrences of serviceWorker (verified by grep 2026-07-21)
nodes.rationale: build.js strips everything inside app.html's BUILD:ONLINE-ONLY fences, notably the service-worker registration: the standalone must never register sw.js (trap #11), otherwise the offline copy would hijack the PWA cache lifecycle of the online app.

nodes.label: Métadonnées PWA sans enregistrement du service worker
nodes.source_location: head: theme-color #12181f, link rel=manifest, apple-touch-icon, apple-mobile-web-app-* metas
nodes.rationale: The PWA <head> metadata is inherited verbatim from app.html by the build, but the standalone carries no serviceWorker registration — installability metadata without the SW lifecycle. Also inherits the non-blocking font-stylesheet trick (media=print swapped at onload) that fixes WebKit's blank first paint.
nodes.norm_label: metadonnees pwa sans enregistrement du service worker

nodes.label: Démarrage autonome (vocabulaire intégré)
nodes.source_location: boot path reads const CARDS directly; the notebook-fetch loader of app.html is replaced by build.js (exact string match on the loader <div>)
nodes.rationale: app.html cannot run as file:// because fetch() of the notebook is blocked; the standalone boots from the embedded CARDS constant instead, which is what makes it a true offline single-file build.

nodes.label: Révision espacée (système de Leitner, localStorage srs_v1)
nodes.norm_label: revision espacee (systeme de leitner, localstorage srs_v1)

nodes.label: Fence BUILD:ONLINE-ONLY (bloc remplacé par build.js dans le standalone)
nodes.norm_label: fence build:online-only (bloc remplace par build.js dans le standalone)

nodes.label: Enregistrement du service worker (app.html, dans la fence ONLINE-ONLY)
nodes.norm_label: enregistrement du service worker (app.html, dans la fence online-only)

nodes.label: Synthèse vocale hébraïque (Web Speech API, sélection de voix par voiceURI)
nodes.norm_label: synthese vocale hebraique (web speech api, selection de voix par voiceuri)

nodes.label: Instantané de session (sessionStorage sess_v1, survit au rechargement)
nodes.norm_label: instantane de session (sessionstorage sess_v1, survit au rechargement)

nodes.label: Les trois modes d'étude (Cartes / Saisie / QCM) aiguillés par render()
nodes.norm_label: les trois modes d'etude (cartes / saisie / qcm) aiguilles par render()

nodes.label: Recherche dynamique dans la banque de mots (accueil)
nodes.norm_label: recherche dynamique dans la banque de mots (accueil)

nodes.label: Clavier hébreu virtuel (3 rangées, finales en doré)
nodes.norm_label: clavier hebreu virtuel (3 rangees, finales en dore)

nodes.label: Remise à zéro du profil (confirmation en deux temps, Annuler par défaut)
nodes.norm_label: remise a zero du profil (confirmation en deux temps, annuler par defaut)

nodes.label: Diagnostic de la voix hébraïque (nom + voiceURI affichés dans les réglages)
nodes.norm_label: diagnostic de la voix hebraique (nom + voiceuri affiches dans les reglages)

nodes.label: Chargement du carnet par fetch() au boot (cache:no-store, DOMParser)
nodes.norm_label: chargement du carnet par fetch() au boot (cache:no-store, domparser)

nodes.label: Niveaux CECRL repliés en quatre libellés (facile/inter/difficile/expert)
nodes.norm_label: niveaux cecrl replies en quatre libelles (facile/inter/difficile/expert)

nodes.label: Portail (index.html) : la porte d'entrée commune aux deux côtés
nodes.norm_label: portail (index.html) : la porte d'entree commune aux deux cotes

nodes.label: Accueil plein écran (ménorahs, א vectoriel, s'efface au clic)
nodes.norm_label: accueil plein ecran (menorahs, א vectoriel, s'efface au clic)

nodes.label: Salut bilingue au hasard (français / hébreu sans nikoud)
nodes.norm_label: salut bilingue au hasard (francais / hebreu sans nikoud)

nodes.label: Fontes Google chargées en non-bloquant (media=print puis bascule onload)
nodes.norm_label: fontes google chargees en non-bloquant (media=print puis bascule onload)

nodes.label: Bloc :root de la charte unifiée (identique carnet/app/portail)
nodes.norm_label: bloc :root de la charte unifiee (identique carnet/app/portail)

nodes.label: Workflow graphe-d'abord (query the graph, don't read the files)
nodes.rationale: Answering from the graph costs ~2.3k tokens vs reading a 2000-line HTML file (10.5x fewer tokens per question, measured 2026-07-20); line anchors drifted four times so graphify re-derives line numbers mechanically.
nodes.norm_label: workflow graphe-d'abord (query the graph, don't read the files)

nodes.label: Régime sous-agents — STANDING DIRECTIVE, délégation maximale
nodes.rationale: Permanent owner instruction (2026-07-21, stated three times): every turn re-sends the whole accumulated context so growth is quadratic; a subagent returns only its conclusion. Test is the ratio: much intermediate output, short verdict -> delegate. Parallel fan-out, numbered criteria in prompts, reuse finished agents, never read transcripts/screenshots in main thread.
nodes.norm_label: regime sous-agents — standing directive, delegation maximale

nodes.label: Les 14 pièges (inline, hors graphe)
nodes.rationale: Each trap was paid for once; listed inline in CLAUDE.md because a graph query only helps an agent who already suspects there is something to know — they must fire before you ask.

nodes.label: Piège 1 : ne jamais éditer flashcards_hebreu.html à la main
nodes.rationale: 100% generated by node build.js from app.html + the notebook; hand edits are erased on the next build.
nodes.norm_label: piege 1 : ne jamais editer flashcards_hebreu.html a la main

nodes.label: Piège 2 : jamais transition:all
nodes.rationale: The shorthand captures outline-* longhands; WebKit pins them to initial values so a focused element renders the UA ring instead of the gold one while still matching :focus-visible. Silently cost 40+ chips their focus ring. Always list animated properties explicitly.

nodes.label: Piège 3 : font-size:22px sur body, 1rem = 16px
nodes.rationale: 22px sits on body never html in all three files, so 1rem is 16px not 22px (measured in WebKit); the false comment caused a 24-value type drift. Moving 22px to html would scale every rem by 1.375 in the notebook AND app.html.

nodes.label: Piège 4 : trois blocs :root du carnet, jamais fusionnés
nodes.rationale: (1) shared design tokens, (2) 8-step type ramp, (3) reading column. The two local blocks are measured values, not preferences: --colonne-large is a floor (widest table 890px) and --colonne is calibrated on real prose advance (6.63px/char), never a digit's width.
nodes.norm_label: piege 4 : trois blocs :root du carnet, jamais fusionnes

nodes.label: Piège 5 : premier bloc :root identique au octet dans les trois fichiers
nodes.rationale: The notebook is the reference; if --bg/--gold change, regenerate icons and update manifest.webmanifest/theme-color.
nodes.norm_label: piege 5 : premier bloc :root identique au octet dans les trois fichiers

nodes.label: Piège 6 : tout nœud hébreu porte lang="he"
nodes.rationale: 100% of 5234 notebook nodes (measured 2026-07-21) and every generated app node. Count must be measured in a browser, not computed: a new entry also creates its generated span.cursive. Without it a screen reader pronounces Hebrew in French phonetics.
nodes.norm_label: piege 6 : tout nœud hebreu porte lang="he"

nodes.label: Piège 7 : jamais d'or sur un état inactif ni de surface dorée au repos
nodes.rationale: Règle de la lampe: the app's Révision du jour card is the single licensed exception; border:1px dashed means only 'nothing here' (.empty), warning boxes use .attention with a solid rule.
nodes.norm_label: piege 7 : jamais d'or sur un etat inactif ni de surface doree au repos

nodes.label: Piège 8 : data-niveau obligatoire sur chaque li et tr
nodes.rationale: build.js fails naming the offending word if any card lacks one (coverage 757/757); the app's tolerance for unclassified cards is a safety net, not a licence.
nodes.norm_label: piege 8 : data-niveau obligatoire sur chaque li et tr

nodes.label: Piège 9 : chaque entrée Noms/Adjectifs/Verbes porte au moins un exemple
nodes.rationale: Verbs need a present-tense sentence; verifie_exemples.js treats a missing ul.exemples as a blocking error.
nodes.norm_label: piege 9 : chaque entree noms/adjectifs/verbes porte au moins un exemple

nodes.label: Piège 10 : bumper VERSION dans sw.js
nodes.rationale: The fetch handler is stale-while-revalidate: without a bump the previous app.html is served once more and the new one only from the second launch — bump whenever a change must reach the device on the very next launch.

nodes.label: Piège 11 : l'enregistrement SW vit dans le bloc BUILD:ONLINE-ONLY
nodes.rationale: The standalone file must not register the service worker (useless offline, invalid on file://); the portal has its own snippet.
nodes.norm_label: piege 11 : l'enregistrement sw vit dans le bloc build:online-only

nodes.label: Piège 12 : outillage WSL — WebKit réel via Playwright, jamais le Chrome système
nodes.rationale: Visual checks run through Playwright + real WebKit (devices['iPhone 16 Pro']), pure logic through jsdom; the system headless Chrome hangs in WSL2.
nodes.norm_label: piege 12 : outillage wsl — webkit reel via playwright, jamais le chrome systeme

nodes.label: Piège 13 : les contrôles émulés iPhone sont aveugles aux défauts desktop
nodes.rationale: The notebook's unbounded reading width (158 chars/line at 1280) survived every critique because mobile viewports bound text on their own. When touching layout, measure at desktop widths too (1440/1280/992/900/768), and prove a desktop fix leaves the phone identical.
nodes.norm_label: piege 13 : les controles emules iphone sont aveugles aux defauts desktop

nodes.label: Piège 14 : une mesure d'émulation ne vaut qu'en A/B, jamais en absolu
nodes.rationale: Paid for 2026-07-20: emulated WebKit measured 329 ms for the study screen's first render; the real iPhone does it in 41 ms and was faster than the dev machine on all three paths. Emulation models layout, not performance — absolute claims must come off the device (Diagnostic de latence block). Mirror of trap 13: there emulation hid a real defect, here it invented one.
nodes.norm_label: piege 14 : une mesure d'emulation ne vaut qu'en a/b, jamais en absolu

nodes.label: Le couplage d'extraction (deux extracteurs miroirs)
nodes.rationale: app.html's extractCards scrapes the notebook DOM; build.js re-implements the same parse with regexes. Edits to the notebook can silently drop cards. Invariants: exact count LABEL text is the key, positional table columns, listCats mirrored in build.js, depth-aware li scanning, identical property insertion order (byte-compared by --check), word's own .he/.fr spans first.

nodes.label: Schéma de carte {cat, he, tr, fr, ...}
nodes.rationale: {cat, he, tr, fr, note?, niveau?, exemples?, he_plain, forms?, genre?}; table-derived cards have tr:'' and the UI falls back to he2tr(card.he).
nodes.norm_label: schema de carte {cat, he, tr, fr, ...}

nodes.label: Standard de translittération maison
nodes.rationale: kh=khaf, ch=het, ts=tsadi, ' = ayin everywhere and alef between vowels, final hey kept, ei for tsere/segol+yud, u/f/k/v never ou/ph/q/w. The notebook is authoritative — don't regenerate hand-written .tr from he2tr; the standard is display-only thanks to trKey folding; edit trKey and he2tr together.

nodes.label: Le rituel après toute modification (7 étapes)
nodes.rationale: build.js, verifie_exemples.js if content changed, verify at the cheapest level that proves it (WebKit only when UI touched), bump SW VERSION if needed, refresh graph only on structural change, update docs, commit in French and push to main.
nodes.norm_label: le rituel apres toute modification (7 etapes)

nodes.label: Règle de recalage du graphe : structurel oui, contenu non
nodes.rationale: /graphify . --update costs ~235k tokens (measured 2026-07-20). Structural change (app.html, build.js, sw.js, index.html, new/renamed file) -> refresh; content-only (vocabulary, examples, .md prose) -> skip and say so in the commit. Bought with the 54-example batch: refreshing anyway cost ~4x the useful work and churned the graph (168 nodes added, 87 removed).
nodes.norm_label: regle de recalage du graphe : structurel oui, contenu non

nodes.label: Le carnet, source unique de vérité
nodes.rationale: vocabulaire_hebreu.html holds all content (grammar + vocabulary, ~6000 lines); there is only one app (app.html's code) and the standalone file is a mechanical projection of both.
nodes.norm_label: le carnet, source unique de verite

nodes.label: La couche PWA (manifest, sw.js, icônes)
nodes.rationale: Installable on iPhone, works offline; start_url points to ./ (the portal, decision of 2026-07-19 — landing directly in flashcards surprised); relative start_url/scope because the site lives under /flashcards-hebreu/; iOS freezes an installed PWA's icon at install time.

nodes.label: Stratégie stale-while-revalidate du service worker
nodes.rationale: App and notebook served from cache then refreshed in background (a content update is visible at the next launch); Google fonts cache-first; only root navigations are mapped to the portal shell; VERSION bump required after strategy/asset/icon changes.

nodes.label: Charte graphique unifiée : le bloc :root de 11 jetons
nodes.rationale: 11 tokens (--bg … --line) byte-identical between notebook, app.html and portal — the notebook is the reference (#12181f / gold #d4a24c); any colour change propagates to all three plus manifest/theme-color/icons.
nodes.norm_label: charte graphique unifiee : le bloc :root de 11 jetons

nodes.label: Le contrat de balisage du carnet (clé d'extraction)
nodes.rationale: Sections found by h2 > span.count LABEL (exact text is the key — renaming detaches the section); tables positional (Verbes >=5 cols, Adjectifs >=4, Noms >=3), lists ul.word-list > li via the listCats map; grammar-only sections have no .count mapping and are intentionally excluded — the way to teach a word without creating a card (hé directionnel).
nodes.norm_label: le contrat de balisage du carnet (cle d'extraction)

nodes.label: Le schéma de carte produit (ARCHITECTURE §3)
nodes.rationale: Property insertion order must stay identical between the two extractors — the embedded snapshot is byte-compared by --check. Phrases category cards get a reduced display (.big-he.phrase) so long sentences wrap.

nodes.label: Niveaux CECRL fins et repli en quatre libellés
nodes.rationale: Notebook stores fine CEFR (data-niveau A1..C2, standard and verifiable); the app folds it to Facile=A1, Intermédiaire=A2-B1, Difficile=B2-C1, Expert=C2 (NIVEAUX table). Classification method: L2 curricula, spoken frequency, concrete-vs-abstract judgment; edge cases resolved downward because the app serves beginners. 757 words: A1 339 / A2 295 / B1 119 / B2 4.
nodes.norm_label: niveaux cecrl fins et repli en quatre libelles

nodes.label: Les exemples en situation (ligne éditoriale)
nodes.rationale: Each example is a written sentence (Hebrew+nikud, tr, French), never audio-only (text remains the vector). Short sentences 3-8 words, present tense, example vocabulary near the word's level (validator tolerates +1 level, alerts at +2 — alerting at +1 drowned the signal). 605 examples; Noms/Adjectifs/Verbes covered 100% and blocking. Batches are written without human proofreading but must pass verifie_exemples.js with 0 errors.

nodes.label: Le lexique du validateur et ses deux garde-fous
nodes.rationale: Built from the cards AND the Hebrew of grammar sections (the real question is 'does the notebook teach it?', not 'is it a card?'). Guard 1: ul.exemples excluded from the sweep, otherwise the check becomes circular. Guard 2: a grammar word can only add an unknown entry at level 0, never lower a known card's level — reusing feed() as-is would have neutralised the level check silently.
nodes.norm_label: le lexique du validateur et ses deux garde-fous

nodes.label: Révision espacée (système de Leitner)
nodes.rationale: Persisted memorisation layer invisible to build.js (pure app state): recordResult called from all answer paths writes to localStorage key srs_v1; good answers climb boxes (SRS_INTERVALS), a failure resets; dueCards feeds the Révision du jour session; refreshSrsUi is the single source of truth for body.has-due.

nodes.label: Identité de carte vocalisée cat|he
nodes.rationale: Was cat|he_plain until 2026-07-20: the plain form created three consonantal-homograph collisions that merged Leitner progression; srsMigrateIds recopies old keys at first boot. Consequence: correcting a nikud changes the card's SRS identity and erases its progression — the cost must be priced before any correction.

nodes.label: Le bloc « Diagnostic de latence » (Réglages avancés)
nodes.rationale: perfReport measures each instrumented gesture in three segments — attente (pointerup->handler), travail, affichage (double rAF) — written to #perf-note after paint capture so it doesn't measure itself; init() decomposes boot into #perf-boot (hidden in the standalone). fmtMs writes a narrow no-break space U+202F before 'ms' which breaks naive text comparison. Kept by owner decision: the only instrument if a slowdown is reported again.
nodes.norm_label: le bloc « diagnostic de latence » (reglages avances)

nodes.label: Garde-fous contre la casse silencieuse
nodes.rationale: Three nets against lost cards: init() warns if an expected category gives 0 cards at load; node build.js fails if an EXPECTED_CATS section is empty (mustReplace anchors fail loudly); build.js --check byte-compares the two extractCards implementations.

nodes.label: Le graphe de connaissance du dépôt (graphify-out/)
nodes.rationale: 335 nodes, 505 edges, 23 communities; deliberately shrank 438->335 when the standalone stopped being duplicated as ~90 function nodes. A snapshot, not a living truth: on contradiction the file wins and the graph is rebuilt. Known limits: ~26 cross-batch id-drift edges discarded at build, and each deployed file appears twice (prose view vs code view) so file-node degree is underestimated.
nodes.norm_label: le graphe de connaissance du depot (graphify-out/)

nodes.label: Accessibilité du carnet (lot du 19/07)
nodes.rationale: The notebook was the visual reference but the functional laggard (no @media, no lang, no motion guard until 2026-07-19). Now acquired: lang=he on 100% of Hebrew nodes, prefers-reduced-motion including scroll-behavior:auto (the app's transition:none would not cover smooth scrolling), 44px coarse-pointer targets, aria-label on search, global gold :focus-visible ring (22 tab stops, 0 without ring), main landmark.

nodes.label: Diagnostic voix : voiceURI porte la qualité, name est localisé
nodes.rationale: WebKit filters getVoices() to default-quality voices on iOS (bug 203689, anti-fingerprinting) — an Enhanced voice will never be usable; name is system-localised ('Carmit (forbedret)' on a Norwegian phone) so loadVoices classifies on voiceURI first (reverse-DNS, never translated). The .voice-id line displays voiceURI permanently as the detector: '-compact' confirms the ceiling, '.enhanced.' would reopen the file.
nodes.norm_label: diagnostic voix : voiceuri porte la qualite, name est localise

nodes.label: Correction des réponses tapées (checkAnswer tolérant)
nodes.rationale: Returns 'exact' | 'almost' | false. he->fr: normFr + frVariants split on /, commas, parentheses, articles. fr->he: real Hebrew (normHe, no nikud) or a French-style transliteration folded by trKey and compared to he2tr(card.he) with small Levenshtein tolerance; 'almost' shows '✓ Presque !' pedagogy. trKey and he2tr must converge to the same canonical form.

nodes.label: Vérification sans navigateur graphique (Playwright WebKit + jsdom)
nodes.rationale: Reference tool for visual rendering is Playwright + real WebKit (Safari's engine, same rendering as the target iPhone) with devices['iPhone 16 Pro']; jsdom boots the standalone for end-to-end logic; throwaway Node harnesses for pure logic; system headless Chrome hangs in WSL2.
nodes.norm_label: verification sans navigateur graphique (playwright webkit + jsdom)

nodes.label: North Star : « Le carnet d'étude du soir »
nodes.rationale: Everything derives from one scene: revising Hebrew at night, by lamplight, in a notebook you love reopening. Ink-night background, parchment text; gold is not decor, it is the lamp's light on what matters. Warmth comes from gold and Hebrew serifs, never from artifice.
nodes.norm_label: north star : « le carnet d'etude du soir »

nodes.label: La règle de la lampe
nodes.rationale: Gold is rare light: primary actions, selection, identity — never more than ~10% of the screen. Practical test before gilding a surface at rest: 'action, selection or identity?'. Applied three times (.part, .tip, .steps li::before) — the notebook now has zero gold surfaces at rest; the app's Révision du jour card is the single licensed exception because it IS the encouraged action. Since 2026-07-20 the rule scales to the whole screen: one lamp lit at a time, chosen by state (body.has-due).

nodes.label: La règle des couches
nodes.rationale: Depth is said first by colour: nuit d'encre -> nuit claire -> carte, each layer edged with a filet. Never skip a layer, never invent a fifth grey. Corollary (measured 2026-07-19): a nested element climbs one layer, it does not dig itself a black veil — no transparent black overlays.

nodes.label: La règle des trois voix
nodes.rationale: Frank Ruhl Libre for Hebrew and big words, Assistant for the tool, JetBrains Mono for transliteration. No fourth family, never serif in buttons.

nodes.label: La règle de la vedette
nodes.rationale: On screen, Hebrew is always larger than its translation and any UI element. If a control visually rivals the Hebrew word, the control is wrong. A hierarchy you cannot see is not a hierarchy (.part-name/.part-he were 1.1px apart — widened, not merged).

nodes.label: La règle de la carte unique
nodes.rationale: Only the flashcard gets a real drop shadow — the object you hold in hand. Panels, chips, fields, quiz choices stay flat (border + tonal layer). Adding a shadow to a second object removes the first one's singularity.
nodes.norm_label: la regle de la carte unique

nodes.label: Le pli (details natif : condense, ne cache pas)
nodes.rationale: One shape, native <details>: Réglages avancés, Catégories, Niveau (screen 1278->874px). A fold condenses: the summary carries the current selection truthfully (beyond two entries, count instead of listing — an ellipsed list lies). Never closes under the user's finger: open/closed decided at load only (open while selection is empty). A title that becomes the fold's row leaves the Title voice.
nodes.norm_label: le pli (details natif : condense, ne cache pas)

nodes.label: Palette Nuit d'encre & Or ancien
nodes.rationale: Blue-tinted night in four tonal layers lit by antique gold (#d4a24c) and read in parchment (#ece7dd); text on gold is always Encre sur or (#1a1206), never white; green/red strictly reserved for answer feedback.

nodes.label: La rampe typographique de 8 pas du carnet
nodes.rationale: --pas-titre 2.3rem … --pas-micro 0.7rem in a second, file-local :root block; replaced 24 distinct sizes whose drift was caused by the false '1rem = 22px' comment. No literal font-size outside the ramp; single named exception: 1.15em on Hebrew in French prose (relative by nature). Also learned by measuring: line-height 1.55 on body because nikud composes below the baseline.
nodes.norm_label: la rampe typographique de 8 pas du carnet

nodes.label: La règle des deux colonnes (--colonne / --colonne-large)
nodes.rationale: 28rem prose column (67 chars/line median) and 56rem frame in a third file-local :root block, implemented via main's padding-inline (not max-width+auto margins, to preserve vertical margin collapsing). Both are measured values: column calibrated on real prose advance 6.63px/char (a digit is 19% wider), frame is a floor (widest table 890px). Cascade trap: main > *:not(.table-wrap) is a specificity floor that silently ignores bare-element overrides.

nodes.label: La voix Title (Assistant 700, 0.84rem, capitales, or)
nodes.rationale: The voice of landmark labels — always gold, always tiny next to their content. Declared uses inventoried (panel section titles, .eyebrow, .sr-cat, .srd-title, plus thead th and .subtheme since the 2026-07-19 charte decision — the charte had a blind spot, not the code). No additional use without a charte decision.

nodes.label: La voix Repère-mono (JetBrains Mono, 0.7rem)
nodes.rationale: The structural landmark that situates within the document (.toc-group-label, .part-num); colour follows the lamp rule case by case. Named exception: Révision du jour LEFT this voice for the display voice because it announces an action, not a setting — ten identically-set panel titles made the encouraged action weigh the same as a toggle.

nodes.label: CTA sous le pouce (sticky « Commencer »)
nodes.rationale: Under pointer:coarse, Commencer is position:sticky only while active AND the only lamp lit (body:not(.has-due) .start:enabled). The sticky follows the lamp, not the button: pinned while cards are due it would reintroduce in time the hierarchy corrected in space. The 'scroll to bottom' test proves nothing — the mid-scroll reading decides.

nodes.label: Les trois registres de « Commencer » (lampe / secondaire / désactivé)
nodes.rationale: Gold is not an attribute of the button but the state 'I am the lamp'. What separates secondary-active from disabled is NOT the border (--card-edge and --line differ by two points per channel, indistinguishable): it is the surface and the text. Never express a disabled state with opacity on a coloured surface — opacity:.4 on a gold gradient kept it gold, translucent and tap-intercepting.
nodes.norm_label: les trois registres de « commencer » (lampe / secondaire / desactive)

nodes.label: Carte « Révision du jour » (composant signature)
nodes.rationale: The only gold-tinted surface at rest, licensed because it IS the action the system wants to encourage daily. body.has-due (set by refreshSrsUi which already knows the due count) makes Commencer yield the light. Its :disabled opacity:.55 was replaced by a full skin — the icon stayed gold, i.e. light on an inactive state; verified 0 gold pixels in WebKit.

nodes.label: Clavier hébreu virtuel (composant signature, bureau seulement)
nodes.rationale: Israeli RTL layout, Frank Ruhl 1.35rem letters, final letters in soft gold — a study object that must stay as readable as a card. Absent on touch (pointer:coarse): the iPhone has its native Hebrew keyboard; the virtual one exists only for physical AZERTY desktop keyboards.
nodes.norm_label: clavier hebreu virtuel (composant signature, bureau seulement)

nodes.label: Les quatre piles de polices normatives, écrites en entier
nodes.rationale: Google fonts are the only network dependency, so offline it is the fallback that renders the nikud — generic serif renders it far worse than David Libre ('ça marche dans l'avion'). Normative stacks are read from the code, not the charte prose: the first drafting of the rule was wrong on two stacks.
nodes.norm_label: les quatre piles de polices normatives, ecrites en entier

nodes.label: Les trois anti-références (Duolingo, SaaS générique, manuel austère)
nodes.rationale: Explicitly rejected: Duolingo gamification (mascots, confetti, streaks), generic SaaS aesthetics (purple gradients, cards everywhere, glassmorphism), austere textbook (dry, grey, intimidating).
nodes.norm_label: les trois anti-references (duolingo, saas generique, manuel austere)

nodes.label: Le pointillé ne dit qu'une chose : « rien ici »
nodes.rationale: border:1px dashed is reserved for .empty (empty section after search filtering); it previously carried two opposite meanings ('nothing here' and 'important, read this') — the exact inverse of a signal. Warnings use .attention with a solid rule. Since 2026-07-21 .empty is actually instantiated (was dead CSS).
nodes.norm_label: le pointille ne dit qu'une chose : « rien ici »

nodes.label: Rangs en cartes (tables de vocabulaire ≤ 640px)
nodes.rationale: Below 640px the 19 vocabulary tables (recognised by :has(ul.exemples)) become full-width cards — labels derived in pure CSS from column count (3=Noms, 4=Adjectifs, 5=Verbes) so the markup the two extractors parse is untouched. The 12 grammar tables keep their grid: their structure IS the lesson. Motive: table{direction:rtl} in an LTR wrap opened all 31 tables on their END on iPhone.

nodes.label: L'anneau :focus-visible doré global
nodes.rationale: outline:2px solid var(--gold), offset 2px, and nothing else — never border-radius on a :focus-visible rule (it would redraw the element while focused, not decorate the ring). Same ring in all three files; app.html is the reference idiom.

nodes.label: TODO « Reprendre ici » (état de reprise entre sessions)
nodes.rationale: The state lives in git and in this section, not in the context window — one session per chantier then /clear. As of 2026-07-21 session 10: no open chantier, audit settled, 757 cards / 605 examples, SW v17, all three on-device confirmations acquired.
nodes.norm_label: todo « reprendre ici » (etat de reprise entre sessions)

nodes.label: Chantier lag iPhone (clos par la mesure on-device)
nodes.rationale: Verdict: no app path is slow on the real device (boot 31 ms, chip 49 ms, session start 82 ms, all under perception threshold); the cold PWA cache was the only explanation left standing, no fix due. Emulation had invented a 329 ms defect the device does in 41 ms. Open lead kept unapplied: 'attente' (iOS click synthesis, 21-29 ms) could be cut via bindTap, but its preventDefault blocks focus — an accessibility risk not worth an imperceptible gain.
nodes.norm_label: chantier lag iphone (clos par la mesure on-device)

nodes.label: Chantier premier affichage (CSS Google Fonts bloquante)
nodes.rationale: The Google Fonts stylesheet was the only blocking external resource: WebKit painted nothing (white screen) until it arrived; three pages requested three different css2 URLs and the fonts.gstatic.com preconnect was missing. Fixed with media='print' onload swap + noscript fallback + preconnect, SW v14. Accepted trade-off: a few hundred ms of fallback fonts before the swap — strictly better than a white screen. The embedded diagnostic cannot see this phase (its clock starts after fonts).
nodes.norm_label: chantier premier affichage (css google fonts bloquante)

nodes.label: Audit complet du carnet (trois phases, soldé)
nodes.rationale: Three sequenced phases (Hebrew accuracy -> pedagogy -> presentation) in multi-agent fan-out with a stop point between each. Four instrument tiers: 0-token script -> optional Haiku -> Sonnet for any Hebrew judgment -> Fable as orchestrator who never reads the slices ('never ask a model what a script decides better and free'). Starts with a calibration (2x10 injected errors, recall threshold 16/20 — a mute control always passes green). SRS cost must be priced before any nikud correction (identity change since cb44367).
nodes.norm_label: audit complet du carnet (trois phases, solde)

nodes.label: Campagne WebKit C1–C12 (22 verdicts, 0 échec)
nodes.rationale: Replayed in three parallel real-WebKit subagents (~167k tokens): P0 proven (19 vocabulary tables as cards without scrolling, 12 grammar grids opening on their START), search 10/10, 28 TOC pills, 0 overflow at six widths, 0 console errors. The push of branch lots-presentation-phase3 to main is what deployed it; confirmed on-device by the owner on 21/07.

nodes.label: Lot santé/sécurité P2+P3 (28 mots, 757 cartes, SW v17)
nodes.rationale: Phase-1 rigour applied in fan-out: material drafting, two-lens counter-expertise (3 errors caught, one suspicion refuted with Academy sources), double dry-run validator control on the JSON before writing (caught defective spellings אָרֹךְ and עַכְשָׁו), SRS cost priced N=0, deterministic insertion script. Content-only: no graph update, no WebKit.
nodes.norm_label: lot sante/securite p2+p3 (28 mots, 757 cartes, sw v17)

nodes.label: Le contrôle à blanc (validateur rejoué sur le JSON avant écriture)
nodes.rationale: The validator's checks are replayed on the batch JSON before a single line enters the source of truth — it stopped three sentences with out-of-notebook vocabulary and the defective spellings that grep misses. Lesson: the dry-run catches what a green post-hoc check would have let in.
nodes.norm_label: le controle a blanc (validateur rejoue sur le json avant ecriture)

nodes.label: TODO § Outillage (WSL, à recréer par session)
nodes.rationale: Playwright browsers persist in ~/.cache/ms-playwright, only the npm lib is reinstalled per session; system libs (libgtk-4-1, libavif13, libgstreamer-plugins-bad1.0-0) installed; iPhone 16 Pro emulation for mobile, classic viewport for desktop.
nodes.norm_label: todo § outillage (wsl, a recreer par session)

links.source_location: line 2 banner: FICHIER GÉNÉRÉ par `node build.js`
line 785: snapshot is the serialized output of build.js's extractor; --check byte-compares it against app.html's extractCards
the loader <div> of app.html is replaced by exact string match in build.js to produce this boot path
absence of navigator.serviceWorker.register: the standalone deliberately does not register sw.js
boot path consumes const CARDS instead of fetching the notebook

hyperedges.label: Le contrat d'extraction — ce que extractCards et build.js scrutent ensemble
Les trois blocs :root du carnet — charte partagée / rampe locale / colonne, jamais fusionnés
Extraction positionnelle des trois tables (Verbes ≥5, Adjectifs ≥4, Noms ≥3 colonnes)
Provenance du build standalone (app.html + carnet → build.js → fichier généré)
Flux de révision espacée (Leitner, srs_v1)
Flux de réponse du mode Saisie (vérification tolérante + verdict corrigeable)
Flux de démarrage (fetch du carnet → extraction → construction de l'accueil)
Les règles nommées de la charte visuelle
Le couplage critique des deux extracteurs et ses gardes
Le workflow d'économie de contexte (graphe + sous-agents + recalage conditionnel)
